codeReview.summary: The refactor does fix the original missing callback forwarding in AgentBridge and the new tests cover that wiring, but the user-visible selection flow is still incomplete. In extension mode, selecting a running scoop still hydrates only from SessionStore, while partial text/tool-start state produced before the click lives only in the offscreen in-memory buffer and is never replayed. That leaves VAL-SPAWN-016 unsatisfied for active runs that already emitted deltas or started a tool before the user selected the scoop.

issues.description: `onResponse()` and `onToolStart()` now receive bridge-scoop events, but they still only update the offscreen in-memory buffer and emit live `agent-event`s; they do not persist the current turn to SessionStore (`onResponseDone()` / `onToolEnd()` / `onSendMessage()` are the only persistence points). Extension `selectScoop()` then loads only `session-${folder}` from IndexedDB and explicitly does no buffer reconciliation (`packages/webapp/src/ui/main.ts:390-394`), while `OffscreenClient.handleAgentEvent()` only forwards events that arrive *after* selection (`packages/webapp/src/ui/offscreen-client.ts:313-360`). Result: if a bridge scoop already emitted partial text or a tool-start before the user clicks its row, those pre-click updates are still invisible, and if the next event is only `response_done`/`ready` the panel can remain empty. The fix needs either immediate persistence of in-progress buffer updates or an explicit replay/hydration path when selecting a scoop.

Each bridge-scoop status transition is now broadcast twice: the AgentBridge extra callback calls `orchestrator.updateBridgeTabStatus(jid, status)`, and that helper already invokes `this.callbacks.onStatusChange(jid, status)` (`packages/webapp/src/scoops/orchestrator.ts:327-333`). `buildForwardingScoopCallbacks()` then forwards the same status again (`packages/webapp/src/scoops/orchestrator.ts:447-450`). In extension mode this duplicates `scoop-status` + `emitScoopList()` traffic (`packages/chrome-extension/src/offscreen-bridge.ts:140-152`); in CLI mode it duplicates the selected-scoop ready callback path (`packages/webapp/src/ui/main.ts:1055-1067`). This looks mostly harmless today, but it is unnecessary churn and makes status-sensitive behavior harder to reason about.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture notes now document callback forwarding as the VAL-SPAWN-016 fix, but they still omit the separate extension-side hydration invariant: the panel loads scoop threads only from `session-${folder}` in IndexedDB, and pre-selection `agent-event`s are not replayed. Without that invariant in shared state, it is easy to stop at forwarding-only changes and miss pre-click output replay.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:119-137 documents `buildForwardingScoopCallbacks` as the authoritative fix, but `packages/webapp/src/ui/main.ts:390-394` loads only SessionStore state and `packages/webapp/src/ui/offscreen-client.ts:313-360` ignores pre-selection events; `packages/chrome-extension/src/offscreen-bridge.ts:84-99,167-180` keeps partial text/tool-start state only in the offscreen buffer until later persistence points.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The current regression coverage validates the callback plumbing pieces separately but does not exercise the actual selection flow 'output/tool-start happened before the click, then the user selects the scoop'. That gap is what allowed this remaining user-visible failure mode to slip through scrutiny.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The new feature tests in `packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-bridge-callback-forwarding.test.ts:147-463` stop at Orchestrator callback spies; existing extension tests in `packages/chrome-extension/tests/offscreen-bridge.test.ts:112-209` and `packages/webapp/tests/ui/offscreen-client.test.ts:94-117,356-379` verify buffering and selected-scoop event handling separately, but not the handoff between them.

summary: Fail. The callback-forwarding refactor is real, but selecting a running bridge scoop can still miss already-produced text/tool-start state because extension selection hydrates only from persisted SessionStore data and the new code still does not persist or replay in-progress buffers. I also found a non-blocking duplicate status-broadcast path.
